results.title: Aligning Eligibility Criteria for Public Transport Vouchers and Community Health Assist Scheme
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed the Government is reviewing Public Transport Voucher (PTV) eligibility criteria and will consider aligning them with the Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) to enhance clarity for residents. This review addresses the suggestion made by Mr Edward Chia Bing Hui to harmonize the two schemes. No specific timeline or outcome for the alignment was stated during the debate. The Ministry will evaluate the proposal further before any changes are implemented.

results.title: Accidents Reported between Pedestrians and Bicycles, PMDs, PMAs and Personal Mobility Assistive Devices
results.summary: From 2019 to 2024, an average of 153 off-road accidents involving active mobility devices occurred annually, comprising 71 bicycle incidents, 66 motorised personal mobility device cases, 13 power-assisted bicycle events, and 15 personal mobility aid occurrences. The Government does not collect data on civil suits filed by pedestrians against these users or the resulting compensation amounts. Consequently, specific figures regarding legal actions and financial settlements remain unavailable.

results.title: Actions Taken to Assist Full-time Teachers' Workload based on OECD TALIS 2024 Report Results
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding hiring administrative staff or piloting contingent teaching assistants to assist secondary school teachers has already been addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 2 to 5 on 4 November 2025. This response covers the Ministry's position on measures to help teachers focus on core teaching duties following the OECD TALIS 2024 report findings. No new actions or timelines were announced in this specific exchange.

results.title: Allowing Bus Service 87 to Stop in Front of Hougang Polyclinic
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority will not allow Bus Service 87 to stop at Hougang Polyclinic despite the route passing nearby. Temporary route changes implemented in 2022 for Cross Island MRT construction will revert to pre-2022 paths only after completion, expected in 2030. Adding a stop would affect six existing services, causing congestion along Hougang Ave 4 and creating safety issues for pedestrians. Consequently, the measure to benefit seniors by avoiding foot access or transfers is not feasible under current operational constraints.

results.title: Application Rates for Operation of Private Heavy Vehicle Parks and Reasons for Rejection
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, an average of 59% of Heavy Vehicle Park licence applications were unsuccessful annually. The Land Transport Authority rejects applications primarily when proposed sites are located within residential areas, layouts fail to adhere to the Vehicle Parking Provision Code of Practice, or applicants lack authorization to operate on the premise. Rejections also occur if the applicant is neither the premise owner nor a tenant, or if supporting documentation is incomplete. These criteria ensure operational compliance and community safety standards are met before granting licences.

results.title: Applications for Caregiver Respite Programme
results.summary: The Take-A-Break (TAB) pilot, administered by SPD and supported by SG Enable since 2020, provides respite services including overnight care. Since inception, 576 applications were received, with 84% approved, 13% withdrawn, and 3% rejected for being outside scope. The Ministry is currently reviewing TAB's plan beyond 31 March 2026. Overnight respite care is available through service providers without requiring a separate extension.

results.title: Assessing Cliff Effect of Healthcare Costs for Households That Marginally Exceed Orange CHAS Subsidy Threshold
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track specific out-of-pocket costs for households marginally exceeding CHAS Orange thresholds but monitors affordability via post-subsidy bills. Based on 2024 data, the 90th percentile annual post-subsidy bill for chronic conditions under CHAS Green is approximately $550, or less than $50 monthly, with further reductions from MediSave 500/700 and Flexi-MediSave. The current three-tier structure for CHAS General Practitioner visits balances subsidy variation with scheme complexity to support different income segments. For individuals who marginally exceed per capita household income thresholds of a better tier, the Ministry will assess their circumstances on a case-by-case basis upon appeal rather than implementing additional graduated subsidy tiers.

results.title: Audit and Verification Mechanisms to Ensure Accuracy of Service Delay Lengths Reported by Rail Operators
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that rail operators must inform commuters of expected service delays within 15 minutes, though these figures are indicative and vary by location. The Rail Reliability Taskforce is studying how to provide more customised delay information, while LTA evaluates new metrics to better reflect disruption impacts for future reliability reports. For regulatory tracking, LTA records only the total incident resolution time, excluding additional traveling time. Between January and August 2025, there were 17 MRT disruptions lasting 9 to 160 minutes and 12 LRT disruptions ranging from 10 to 300 minutes. The Minister did not provide a full list of all 2025 delays or confirm immediate integration of passenger impact into current performance measurements.

results.title: Automated Bus Ramps and Infrastructural Upgrades for Autonomous Vehicles
results.summary: Autonomous buses for public bus services 400 and 191 pilots feature wheelchair bays and deployable low ramps, while eight-seater Roboshuttles in Punggol accommodate folded wheelchairs without ramps. Onboard safety operators will assist wheelchair users during these pilots, with future operations contingent on data collected by the Land Transport Authority regarding accessibility needs when operators are absent. Autonomous buses will utilize existing barrier-free bus stops, and the Ministry is collaborating with manufacturers to evaluate future infrastructure requirements such as sensors or dedicated pick-up points. The impact of these infrastructure changes on existing road users remains under assessment based on pilot data.

results.title: Average PSLE Aggregate Scores for Lowest Performing National Primary Schools and Madrasahs
results.summary: From 2021 to 2024, the average PSLE aggregate score for the six lowest-performing national primary schools remained stable at 22. The four designated madrasahs under the Compulsory Education Act have consistently met the PSLE benchmark, which is pegged to the average score of Malay pupils in those six schools taking four Standard-level subjects. The Minister confirmed that all four madrasahs satisfied this requirement since 2021. No further data or changes to these metrics were reported during the session.

results.title: Breakdown of New COE Registrations Awarded to Existing Holders versus Recent and First-time Owners in 2025
results.summary: In the first three quarters of 2025, approximately 24,000 Certificates of Entitlement (COEs) across Categories A and B were awarded to existing holders, representing around 80% of total registrations. Conversely, about 500 COEs (2%) went to recent holders with a COE within the last 60 days, while roughly 6,000 COEs (20%) were awarded to those without a COE in that period. These figures exclude COEs registered to companies and reflect the distribution among private vehicle owners.

results.title: Budget Allocation and Curriculum Review for Character and Citizenship Education Programmes
results.summary: The Ministry of Education designs the Character and Citizenship Education (CCE) curriculum based on age-appropriate moral and social-emotional indicators across six content areas: mental health, national, family, cyber wellness, career guidance, and sexuality education. Implemented through classroom lessons and out-of-classroom activities like Co-Curricular Activities, the full curriculum undergoes a comprehensive review every six to eight years, with sub-components reviewed as needed to address societal trends and student needs. While the Ministry consults educators, parents, and experts to ensure relevance, it does not track or disclose specific expenditure proportions for CCE, noting that subject spending cuts across multiple departments. Consequently, no data is available regarding the proportion of total CCE expenditure spent on vendor engagement or the typical budget allocation per school for these programmes.

results.title: COEs Not Registered to Vehicles After Successful Bids
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, an average of 650 (1%) temporary COEs issued for successful bids remained unused for vehicle registration. The Acting Minister for Transport stated this low expiry rate indicates dealers are not speculating but bidding based on actual demand. Current validity periods already range from one month for Category D to six months for Categories A and B, which are shorter than the one-year period queried. The Ministry noted these existing shorter durations address concerns about front-running and speculative bidding.

results.title: Casino Attendance Changes in 2024 versus 2023 and Reasons for Reduced Casino Visits
results.summary: In 2024, unique casino visits rose by 11% to 1.4 million, driven by foreign visitors accounting for over 90% of total visitorship. Conversely, visits by Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents declined by approximately 5%, falling from 99,000 to 94,000. The Ministry of Home Affairs stated it is not aware of the underlying reasons for this specific decrease in local resident visits.

results.title: Collecting and Analysing Income Levels of Respondents in Future Digital Parenting Surveys
results.summary: The Minister for Digital Development and Information stated that income is a sensitive variable respondents typically decline to disclose, making direct collection and analysis in subsequent Digital Parenting Surveys unfeasible. Consequently, uneven response rates regarding income do not aid data analysis, prompting survey designers to evaluate whether proxies, such as educational attainment, suffice for representativeness. Findings from the current survey indicate that parents with secondary or lower educational qualifications were less likely to check their child's online activities or guide their digital behavior. The government will assess if these proxies adequately reflect the resident parent population's income distribution without compromising data quality.

results.title: Common Reasons Given by Teachers for Leaving from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: Over the past five years, the most frequently cited reasons for teacher resignations are family needs, including caregiving and childcare, and pursuing other career interests. The Minister for Education confirmed these factors as the primary drivers for leaving the profession during this period. No specific statistics, timelines, or alternative measures were provided in the response.

results.title: Comparison Rate between Non-ComLink+ Families and Returning Families Previously under ComLink+
results.summary: Fifty-four percent of ComCare Short-to-Medium Term Assistance (SMTA) households that exited in 2020 returned within 36 months, per the Ministry of Social and Family Development's Supporting Lower-Income Households Trends Report 2024. A specific return rate for ComLink+ families is not available because the programme was only rolled out to eligible families in 2023. The report is accessible at https://go.gov.sg/msflowerincomereport2024.

results.title: Complaints Received and Timeline of Discovery of Inappropriate Clauses within Severance Agreements by Tripartite Partners
results.summary: Tripartite partners became aware of inappropriate clauses in Agoda's August 2025 severance agreements through media reports, as the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not proactively monitor private contracts. MOM, TAFEP, and the National Trades Union Congress swiftly engaged Agoda following the September 2025 reports, leading to a public apology and employee outreach. Over the last three years, MOM received no other complaints regarding clauses discouraging workers from approaching authorities or unions. While the Government avoids over-regulating private agreements, it maintains that provisions inhibiting access to redress are unacceptable under any circumstances. Affected workers may approach MOM or TAFEP for assistance with unfair or illegal clauses, and MOM will take action against errant employers where necessary.

results.title: Complaints on Car Dealership In-house Financing Schemes Lodged in Past Five Years and Expanding Motor Vehicle Financing Regulation to Include In-house Schemes
results.summary: Over the past five years, MAS received an average of five annual complaints regarding disputes involving in-house car financing schemes offered by unregulated car dealers. The Monetary Authority clarified that these dealers are not financial institutions and thus fall outside current motor vehicle financing rules, which primarily apply to licensed financial institutions, exempt moneylenders, and hire-purchase entities. While MAS noted the existing regulatory framework is generally effective as most borrowers use regulated lenders, it strongly advises consumers to avoid unregulated schemes due to risks like hidden charges and potential borrower losses upon default.

results.title: Considerations and Efforts Taken to Increase Usage of $500 SkillsFuture Credits before its Expiry End-2025
results.summary: The Government will not extend the expiry of the one-off $500 SkillsFuture Credit top-up for eligible Singaporeans aged 25 and above, which ends in 2025, as the limited validity was intended to encourage timely upskilling. As of end-September 2025, approximately 800,000 individuals, or three in ten eligible Singaporeans, have utilized these credits, with mid-career workers aged 40 to 60 comprising nearly half of this group. While employer-sponsored training in 2024 covered about 241,000 individuals, reducing the need for personal credit usage, the Ministry continues to lower learning barriers through flexible e-learning modalities and enhanced MySkillsFuture portal features. The Ministry will not lower subscription prices or extend the deadline but will guide learners toward suitable courses targeting in-demand skills like AI.

results.title: Consumer Protection Measures for Seniors against High-value Beauty and Hair Salon Package Deals
results.summary: The Consumer Protection (Fair Trading) Act prohibits unfair practices like undue pressure on all consumers, with recourse available via CASE or the Small Claims Tribunals. An independent Consumer Protection Review Panel, led by CASE President Mr Melvin Yong and former judicial commissioner Ms Foo Tuat Yien, was convened in March 2025 to review concerns including pressure sales tactics. The Panel is expected to publish findings and recommendations by end-2026. The Government will study these recommendations to determine if additional measures, such as mandatory cooling-off periods for high-value packages sold to seniors, are required. No immediate mandatory cooling-off period has been imposed.

results.title: Criteria for Heritage Tree Conservation and Infrastructure Development and Increasing Frequency of Heritage Trees Inspection
results.summary: NParks assesses heritage trees holistically based on public safety, health condition, girth size, and cultural, historical, and social value when balancing conservation with infrastructure development. Inspections occur on a half-yearly basis, supplemented by detailed diagnostic checks and continuous monitoring technology where necessary. The Minister confirmed these criteria apply to decisions regarding preservation or removal, such as the recent case of a decayed Angsana tree in Potong Pasir. No change to the standard half-yearly inspection frequency was proposed despite the recent removal.

results.title: Current Escalation Protocol for Breaches in Care and Protection Orders Involving Children
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development's Protective Service (PSV) triggers an immediate risk reassessment upon any breach of a Care and Protection Order (CPO). If high risk is identified, PSV may exercise statutory powers to remove the child from the home or refer the case to the Police for suspected offences. The matter can also be brought before the Youth Court for review, where additional safety conditions such as supervised contact, counselling, or psychotherapy may be imposed. These measures aim to address immediate risks, particularly when children are placed in alternative care, ensuring their safety through direct intervention or legal enforcement.

results.title: Data on Annual Deaths and Chronic Health Problems Resulting from Secondhand Smoking in Past Five Years
results.summary: Based on the Global Burden of Disease 2023 study, secondhand smoke contributes approximately 1% of Singapore's total disease burden, measured by years of healthy life lost. The estimated annual deaths attributable to secondhand smoke were 364 in 2019, 355 in 2020, 368 in 2021, 382 in 2022, and 404 in 2023. These figures represent premature deaths and disability resulting from exposure to secondhand smoke over the past five years.

results.title: Data on Applications and Approvals for Tax Incentives under Section 13U of Income Tax Act 1947 for Last 10 Years
results.summary: The Minister stated that MAS has ceased tax incentives for two Single Family Office (SFO) funds linked to sanctioned individuals following enforcement operations on 30 October 2025, while investigations into Chen Zhi and associates remain ongoing. Data indicates that approximately 3% of the 1,300 SFO applications over the past three years were rejected, with additional withdrawals occurring during the pre-application screening stage against databases for illegal activities. Effective immediately, all SFOs must maintain MAS-licensed bank accounts, notify MAS upon commencing operations, and submit annual returns to support surveillance efforts. The government maintains a risk-proportionate approach rather than zero-risk, noting that SFOs linked to convicted money launderers represent less than 1% of the sector to balance integrity with competitiveness.

results.title: Data on Employers Reducing Scope of Work-from-home Policies
results.summary: The proportion of employers offering scheduled tele-working declined from 56.5% in 2021 to 38.4% in 2024, indicating a scaling back of pandemic-era policies as employees returned on-site. However, overall flexible work arrangements (FWAs) increased, with 72.7% of employers providing at least one type in 2024 compared to 52.7% in 2019. Additionally, the variety of FWAs expanded, and over 2,100 employers have adopted the Tripartite Standard on Work-Life Harmony since its April 2021 launch. These figures suggest a shift from universal tele-working to more diverse, need-based flexible arrangements.

results.title: Data on Individual COE Ownership and Breakdown of Ownership Distribution by Individual and Household Size in Last 10 Years
results.summary: Over the last 10 years, approximately 800,000 unique individuals held a Certificate of Entitlement (COE), excluding company registrations. About 5% of these individuals owned multiple cars sequentially or concurrently, while 15% of car-owning households held multiple vehicles. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that adjusting ownership distribution by household size on a per capita basis is not meaningful due to varying household needs and usage extending beyond immediate family members. Consequently, no per capita breakdown data is provided.

results.title: Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Corporate and Accounting Laws (Amendment) Bill 2025 seeks to tighten rules against company misuse, safeguard shareholders, and enhance public accountant accountability while reducing regulatory burdens. Key amendments clarify registered office hours, reporting timelines for position-holder changes, financial reporting simplifications, and confirm that data protection scope does not expand via electronic access. Ms Indranee Rajah noted most provisions commence after six months to allow preparation, addressing SME concerns about disproportionate penalties and compliance capacity. Mr Lee Hong Chuang emphasized the need for proportionate safeguards, such as masking addresses, and grace periods for unintentional breaches to prevent excessive burdens on SMEs without dedicated compliance staff. The debate balances strengthening governance with maintaining Singapore's status as a trusted business hub by removing outdated rules and fostering a vibrant environment.

results.title: Data on Mid-careerists Transitioning to Care Economy and Job Retention Rates and Challenges
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, approximately 1,600 individuals transitioned into the care economy via Workforce Singapore's Career Conversion Programmes, while nearly 1,700 completed SkillsFuture Career Transition Programmes. Over 80% of Career Conversion Programme participants remained employed within the sector 24 months post-hiring, and more than three-quarters of SkillsFuture trainees secured placements in their trained fields. Challenges persist in attracting mid-career workers and facilitating role adaptation, prompting sector agencies to establish structured career pathways, review salary structures, and enhance staff welfare. To support transitions, agencies are implementing outreach, mentorship, course previews, and learning visits, alongside engaging Volunteer Career Advisors to guide applicants through the transition process.

results.title: Data on Muslim and Muslim Convert Marriages by Gender from January 2000 to October 2025
results.summary: From 2000 to 2024, Singapore recorded an average of approximately 20,700 civil marriages and 4,900 Muslim marriages annually via the Registry of Marriages. The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that the Ministry does not track or publish data on these marriages broken down by gender, nor does it maintain specific statistics for recent Muslim converts. Consequently, the requested detailed breakdown by gender for both general Muslim marriages and those involving recent converts remains unavailable in official records.

results.title: Data on Outcomes of Work Injury Claims Made by Platform Workers under Work Injury Compensation Act 2019 since January 2025
results.summary: From January to September 2025, 1,029 work injury claims were lodged by platform workers under the Work Injury Compensation Act 2019. Of these, 752 claims received compensation, 19 were rejected, and 173 remain under processing, while 85 claims were withdrawn by the claimants. The Minister for Manpower provided these statistics during the parliamentary debate. No further breakdown or future projections regarding these claims were stated in the transcript.

results.title: Data on Private Companies Offering Parent-care Leave
results.summary: According to the Ministry of Manpower's 2024 survey, approximately 29.3% of private companies with at least 25 employees, totaling around 5,000 firms, currently offer paid parent-care leave. The Ministry will continue to study the feasibility of enhancing leave provisions and potentially mandating such benefits to better support caregivers. This review aims to balance diverse caregiving needs with employers' operational requirements while ensuring caregivers can sustainably balance work and family responsibilities. No specific timeline for any mandatory changes was stated.

results.title: Data on Retail Food Establishment Openings and Closures in 2025 and Breakdown of Closures Categorised by Years of Operation
results.summary: From 1 January 2025 to 23 October 2025, 3,357 new retail food establishments opened while 2,431 closed. The Minister provided a breakdown of closure durations by years of operation, categorized into five groups: less than three years, three to five years, five to 10 years, 10 to 20 years, and more than 20 years. Specific figures for each duration category are detailed in Table 1 of the official response.

results.title: Data on Sanctioned Individuals Operating Tax-exempt Family Offices Locally
results.summary: MAS identified two Single Family Office funds linked to US-sanctioned individuals and has ceased their tax incentives following enforcement actions on 30 October 2025. The Monetary Authority of Singapore maintains a risk-proportionate approach, rejecting approximately 3% of applications over the past three years while requiring all SFOs to maintain bank accounts subject to customer due diligence. MAS will not adopt a zero-risk stance but will continue to streamline processes without compromising high standards, ensuring ongoing surveillance through Suspicious Transaction Reports and international collaboration. The government emphasizes that combating financial crime requires global effort rather than shutting down the financial hub to avoid incidents.

results.title: Data on Silver Generation Office's Outreach Efforts to Socially Isolated Seniors
results.summary: Over the past three years, the Silver Generation Office engaged nearly 600,000 seniors, identifying approximately 14,000 at risk of social isolation. These individuals are connected to community resources including Active Ageing Centres, where more than 80% of at-risk seniors are engaged through programmes, Healthier SG, long-term care, or activities by the People's Association and Health Promotion Board. Community befrienders provide monthly contact for seniors who accept befriending services, reflecting a whole-of-society approach to addressing isolation.

results.title: Data on Suspected Foreign Interference Reports Received by ISD in Past Five Years and Measures to Increase Awareness and Vigilance
results.summary: The Government does not disclose statistics on suspected foreign interference reports received by the Internal Security Department due to national security reasons. To enhance public vigilance, the Ministry of Home Affairs launched a campaign earlier this year on deepfakes and inauthentic online posts, leveraging the May 2025 General Election to highlight stakes. Future efforts will focus on everyday contexts, supported by the Ministry of Defence's National Education initiatives and the SG101 website, which consolidates educational resources on national positions. Additional programmes like the National Library Board's S.U.R.E. initiative equip citizens with critical thinking skills to evaluate information reliability. The Government continues to conduct briefings and workshops for public servants, national servicemen, and students to build a discerning public as the best defence against foreign interference.

results.title: Deployment of Private Credit Growth Fund Given Rising Global Private-credit Risks
results.summary: The Private Credit Growth Fund (PCGF) will proceed despite GIC's commercial caution regarding global private-credit risks, as the regional market remains less mature than in the US or Europe. Enterprise Singapore will collaborate with the appointed fund manager to ensure investments align with developmental objectives for high-growth enterprises requiring tailored financing beyond traditional instruments. The fund manager retains independent commercial discretion to assess deal merits, prevailing market conditions, and risk-reward profiles when determining the pace and level of capital deployment.

results.title: Differentiating Lorry Speed Limits by Weight Class and Increasing Awareness of Grants to Promote Installation of Speed Limiters
results.summary: Current regulations already differentiate speed limits by weight: light goods vehicles (MLW ≤3,500 kg) are capped at 70 km/h, heavy goods vehicles (MLW >3,500 kg) at 60 km/h, and cement mixers at 40 km/h. The Traffic Police assess this approach as sufficient for road safety and see no need for further differentiation. Regarding financial support, the Productivity Solutions Grant for speed limiter installation is available between 1 October 2025 and 31 March 2027. The Traffic Police are actively engaging the industry on these requirements and grant availability, with no additional measures proposed at this time.

results.title: Disparity between Increased Use of AI and Unchanged Working Hours for Teachers
results.summary: The Minister for Education referred inquiries regarding the 75% AI adoption rate among teachers and unchanged weekly work hours of 47 since 2018 to a previous response delivered on 4 November 2025. That prior response, which addresses the Teaching and Learning International Survey (TALIS) 2024 findings and the management of teachers' workload and well-being, will be published on the Ministry of Education's website. The current transcript does not contain new data, specific timelines for workload reduction, or confirmation of whether teacher workload reduction is now a Key Performance Indicator alongside learning outcomes.

results.title: Duration of Vacancy of Flats under Lease Buyback Scheme and under Relaunch of SBF Exercise in Past 10 Years
results.summary: HDB spruces repossessed Lease Buyback Scheme flats for up to six months before offering them in the next Sale of Balance Flats exercise. The median, average, and longest vacancy durations over the past 10 years were not provided in the response. Additionally, the Minister did not disclose whether HDB maintains internal Key Performance Indicators specifically tracking the vacancy period of these repossessed units.

results.title: Dwell Times for Trains at Interchange and Non-interchange Stations
results.summary: Average MRT and LRT dwell times range from 35 to 60 seconds at interchange stations and 30 to 50 seconds at non-interchange stations, varying based on operating conditions and passenger loading. The Acting Minister for Transport stated these durations have remained largely constant over the past decade to ensure safe boarding and alighting for all passengers, including those with mobility aids. Current dwell times are comparable to metro systems in Taipei and Hong Kong. The Minister did not specify a timeline for reducing these times, noting that reductions are constrained by the need to accommodate all commuters safely.

results.title: Effectiveness of Measures to Address Startup Costs, Rental Pressures and Structural Disadvantages Facing Smaller SMEs and F&B Businesses
results.summary: The Government supports smaller F&B SMEs through the Enterprise Financing Scheme (EFS) Working Capital Loan, a 50% Corporate Income Tax rebate for Assessment Years 2024 and 2025, and the Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) for equipment and digital systems. Since December 2021, $1.26 billion in CDC Vouchers have been spent to boost heartland business sales. New initiatives include the FoodX programme for shared resources and the F&B Process Optimisation Programme (POP) for process review starting in the second half of 2026. Additionally, a redesigned SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit will provide a fresh $10,000 to employers from the second half of 2026 to offset workforce transformation costs via an online wallet, easing cash flow concerns. The Government continues to review measures to ensure relevance for smaller SMEs.

results.title: Effectiveness of Singapore Red Cross' Home Monitoring and Eldercare System and Outreach Strategies to Encourage Installation
results.summary: The Ministry of Health will introduce a 24/7 fall and movement monitoring service under the Enhanced Home Personal Care Service, while HDB extends wireless Alert Alarms to approximately 170 public rental blocks. HDB partners with commercial vendors to offer optional assistive-living technologies for seniors in HDB flats and is open to assessing the feasibility of including the Singapore Red Cross' HoME+ system in this programme. These measures aim to support ageing in place through detection and monitoring technologies.

results.title: Employers' Acceptance of Mental Health-related Medical Certificates
results.summary: Employers are required to accept and grant statutory sick leave for all valid medical certificates, including those for mental health conditions. Doctor-issued certificates must respect patient confidentiality and generally should not disclose the nature of the condition without patient consent. Upon the Workplace Fairness Act's implementation, employers will be prohibited from making adverse employment decisions based on mental health conditions or other protected characteristics. These measures collectively aim to prevent discrimination and ensure fair treatment for individuals with mental health needs in the workplace.

results.title: Enforcing Stricter Border Control Measures to Curb Illegal Creative Freelancing Activities
results.summary: The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) currently verifies entry eligibility based on valid documents and risk assessments for threats like terrorism or trafficking, refusing entry to those suspected of illegal work. Actual cases of illegal freelancing are handled by the Ministry of Manpower, which repatriates offenders and imposes re-entry bans. The Minister clarified that ICA's primary mandate does not include verifying the specific purpose of media equipment beyond general risk criteria. No new stricter border control measures to specifically target creative freelancers were announced during the session.

results.title: Ensuring Adequate CRU Resources, Training and Capacity for Egregious Case Prosecution After Tampines Pilot Programme
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding CRU resources, training, and capacity for egregious case prosecution following the Tampines pilot programme was already addressed in the Ministry's written reply dated 14 October 2025. The transcript does not contain the specific details of that reply, including any new measures, timelines, or eligibility criteria for the pilot programme. Consequently, the summary cannot provide further factual information on the operational constraints or stated actions for the Community Relations Unit beyond the reference to the prior written response.

results.title: Ensuring Admission Scores for Engineering Undergraduate Programmes Sufficiently Meet Programme's Rigours
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) establishes minimum eligibility criteria for autonomous universities (AUs) but does not prescribe specific admission scores for engineering or comparable programmes. Each AU retains autonomy to determine its own admissions metrics and programme requirements. The Ministry closely tracks graduate outcomes by programme to monitor performance. No guidelines were stated for AUs to adopt uniform admission requirements for degrees of similar intellectual rigour.

results.title: Ensuring Age-appropriate Public Playgrounds Near Preschools
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board plans playgrounds in Build-To-Order projects for children aged two to five and five to 12 years. Playgrounds for the younger group (two to five years) are typically sited near preschools, while some areas feature co-located facilities for both age groups. The Minister confirmed this planning approach addresses the need for age-appropriate public playgrounds near preschools.

results.title: Ensuring Equitable Access to AI Tools and Digital Teaching Support Across Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education provides centralised funding and infrastructure support, including wireless networks, computing devices, and software, to ensure all schools can access AI tools and digital teaching resources. Students across primary to pre-university levels utilise the Singapore Student Learning Space (SLS), which offers curriculum-aligned resources and specific AI-enabled features such as the Speech Evaluation Tool, Learning Feedback Assistants, and an Adaptive Learning System. These measures are designed to support learning in a safe and customised manner for all students, regardless of their school's resource profile.

results.title: Evaluation for Professional Development Progammes for Novice Teachers
results.summary: Novice teachers rate professional development programmes on content relevance, delivery quality, and effectiveness, providing direct feedback to the Ministry of Education twice during their first three years of teaching. Schools additionally evaluate mentoring effectiveness through mentor-mentee feedback collected via focused group discussions, surveys, and mentoring logs. The Ministry of Education utilizes this data to review and refine professional development programmes, while also supporting schools with resources and guidance for data analysis to improve school-based mentoring initiatives.

results.title: Evaluation of Lung Cancer Screening Pilot Programme Effectiveness
results.summary: The Ministry of Health currently recommends lung cancer screening only for high-risk individuals, defined as heavy smokers or those with a history of heavy smoking, based on Screening Test Review Committee (STRC) guidelines. These recommendations integrate local and international evidence regarding clinical and cost-effectiveness. Ongoing local studies are evaluating screening outcomes; future reviews will incorporate published results to potentially update eligibility criteria. No immediate changes to the screening programme are indicated pending these study outcomes.

results.title: Exempted Business
results.summary: The debate on exempted business was interrupted at 7.00 pm due to the number of speakers remaining. Ms Indranee Rajah moved to proceed beyond this time to complete the discussion. No specific policy measures, timelines, or eligibility rules were stated in this segment. The transcript contains only procedural information regarding the extension of the sitting hour.

results.title: Expanding Criteria Governing Outfit of Existing Pedestrian Overhead Bridges with Lifts to Include Hawker Centres
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority prioritises installing lifts at pedestrian overhead bridges (POBs) with high footfall and significant senior usage, which typically include locations near key amenities like hawker centres. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that existing criteria already account for these factors, indicating no immediate plan to formally expand the eligibility list beyond current operational priorities. Consequently, the specific request to broaden criteria for hawker centre-adjacent bridges is not supported by a stated change in policy at this time.

results.title: Expanding Extended Producer Responsibility to Textiles and Fast Fashion and Reducing Burden on Consumers
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) currently covers e-waste (implemented 2021) and beverage containers (introduced next year), with no immediate expansion to textiles. Used textiles are deemed not readily recyclable due to varied materials and are managed via in-store collection for resale or reuse. The Ministry will continue reviewing ways to support textile waste management, maintaining that proper waste management remains a shared responsibility involving reduction, reuse, and recycling by all stakeholders.

results.title: Expanding Save-A-Live Initiative to Landed Property Estates with Installation of AEDs
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) is expanding the Save-A-Life initiative to install Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs) in landed property estates, with 13 estates already equipped. The Ministry intends to extend this coverage to additional estates over time. Community organisations wishing to install AEDs may approach SCDF, which will facilitate the process with relevant agencies. This collaborative effort aims to strengthen emergency preparedness and response capabilities within landed residential areas.

results.title: Expected Off-peak Travel Take-up Rate in Light of Free Off-peak Travel Commencing
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that details regarding the expected off-peak travel take-up rate, additional resource requirements for SBS Transit before 7.30 am, and annual revenue leakage for the free off-peak travel scheme commencing 27 December 2025 were already addressed at the 4 November 2025 Parliament Sitting. No new figures or timelines were provided in this session. The Minister did not elaborate on the long-term sustainability of the initiative or confirm if further resources are needed prior to the launch date.

results.title: Factors Contributing to Early Seasonal Surge of Influenza Cases and Prevention Strategies
results.summary: Influenza activity in Singapore typically peaks between May–August and December–March, though timing varies annually due to international travel, weather, immunity levels, and population mixing patterns. The early surge since August 2025 reflects this natural variability rather than a fundamental shift in transmission cycles. The Communicable Diseases Agency continuously enhances surveillance at primary care clinics and acute hospitals to detect surges. Under the National Childhood and Adult Immunisation Schedules, vulnerable groups are recommended annual or per-season vaccination, available year-round. Medical practitioners receive bi-annual clinical guidance on administration. Individuals should consult doctors to determine their specific need and timing for vaccination based on individual circumstances, as influenza transmits throughout the year.

results.title: Faraid Inheritance Rights for Muslim Children Born out of Wedlock under Administration of Muslim Law Act
results.summary: Under the Administration of Muslim Law Act, a Muslim child born out of wedlock is eligible for Faraid inheritance only if the deceased is the child's biological mother or a maternal relative. The Legal (Fatwa) Committee, chaired by the Mufti, ruled that such children are not eligible to inherit from their biological father or any paternal relative. This distinction applies strictly to biological lineage and does not extend to paternal lines regardless of the circumstances of birth.

results.title: Formal Accreditation or Integration of GRaduate Industry Traineeship Programme with Existing Academic Programmes at IHLs
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated there are no plans to formally accredit or integrate the GRaduate Industry Traineeship (GRIT) Programme with existing academic training at Institutes of Higher Learning. GRIT is designed as a temporary initiative to support fresh graduates' entry into the workforce, with placements lasting three to six months. Consequently, the Ministry will not incorporate best practices from IHL internship programmes into the GRIT curriculum to align with academic standards.

results.title: Future Plans Towards Education and Training Initiatives Based on SkillsFuture's Success and Effectiveness
results.summary: The Minister stated there are no immediate plans to top up the broad-based $500 SkillsFuture Credit, noting a recent $4,000 mid-career top-up was provided last year for Singaporeans aged 40 and above. Instead, the government will continue calibrating support between broad-based credits and targeted measures, including up to 90% course fee subsidies for industry-relevant programmes and incentives for Work Study Programmes. Evaluation of training impact relies on employment and wage outcomes, such as the 6% to 11% wage premiums observed for ITE and polytechnic graduates upgrading via Work-Study, alongside learner ratings published on the MySkillsFuture portal. The Ministry will monitor diverse training needs to balance individual-led and employer-led initiatives without pivoting to alternative support forms at this time.

results.title: Guidelines for Mall Operators to Allocate for Short-term Delivery-driver Parking
results.summary: In 2022, the Urban Redevelopment Authority and Land Transport Authority issued guidelines for mall operators to allocate short-term parking for delivery motorcycles and bicycles. Developed by a tripartite committee, these measures include setting aside specific waiting bays and providing a 15 to 20-minute grace period for riders to complete pick-ups. The guidelines were formulated based on feedback from focus groups involving delivery riders, mall owners, and platform companies to ensure safe and efficient operations.

results.title: Impact of Red-Amber-Green Arrows at Traffic Junctions
results.summary: Since 2018, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has installed Red-Amber-Green (RAG) arrows at over 1,200 junctions, reducing right-turn accidents by approximately 40%. While this safety measure is effective, right-turn waiting times at sampled locations increased by 20 to 40 seconds, representing a 30% to 50% rise depending on junction characteristics and time of day. LTA assesses these impacts through comprehensive traffic monitoring, data analysis, and simulation.

results.title: Impact on OneMillionTrees Movement When Trees Are Lost from Redevelopment or Infrastructure Works
results.summary: The OneMillionTrees movement, launched in April 2020, aims to plant one million trees by 2030 to enhance landscapes and ecological connectivity. Trees planted under this initiative are distinct from existing greenery replacement measures used to offset clearances for redevelopment or infrastructure works. Consequently, large-scale tree losses from such projects are not deducted from the movement's planting tally. Success is driven by community stewardship rather than net canopy metrics, with separate mechanisms handling offset planting for development-induced clearances.

results.title: Inclusion of Mental Health Screening in Grow Well SG Programme's Child Health Plan
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that routine mental health screening for asymptomatic individuals is not included in the Child Health Plan under Grow Well SG due to a lack of strong scientific evidence. Singapore instead adopts a risk-based approach focusing on early identification and intervention for those with existing concerns. Support is provided through trained peer supporters, educators, and staff in schools and Institutes of Higher Learning who can identify distress and refer students to community mental health professionals for assessment and intervention.

results.title: Involving Students in Food Preparation to Promote Independence and Sense of Community in View of Central Kitchen Model in 2026
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that schools currently foster independence and community spirit through existing programmes like Outdoor Adventure Learning camps and Co-Curricular Activities. Regarding the upcoming central kitchen model in 2026, the Ministry confirmed it does not intend to involve students in food procurement, preparation, or distribution to achieve these objectives. Consequently, the proposed measures to integrate students into canteen operations will not be implemented.

results.title: Justification for Higher Fare Increase for Express Buses and Potential Impacts of Pricing Strategy on Ridership
results.summary: The Public Transport Council (PTC) increased express bus fares by up to $0.50, compared to $0.10 for basic services, to close a cost gap that had persisted since 2010. This adjustment reflects that unidirectional express bus unit costs are approximately 1.5 times higher than basic services. The PTC acknowledged potential impacts on commuters but noted that the Land Transport Authority will continue providing fare rebates via the Travel Smart Journeys programme to encourage uptake for services managing rail crowding, such as City Direct Services. The debate did not specify exact ridership parameters or a detailed formula used for the calculation.

results.title: Lessons Learnt and New Processes following Review into Megan Khung Case
results.summary: Minister Desmond Lee accepted the Review Panel's findings regarding the February 2020 fatal child abuse case, acknowledging lapses by the Child Protective Service (CPS) and Singapore Police Force (SPF) that missed opportunities to intervene. The Ministry will implement reforms across three pillars: enhancing frontline social worker capabilities, strengthening systemic oversight with real-time case tracking, and aligning inter-agency coordination among CPS, SPF, ECDA, and social service agencies. Specific measures include establishing a central coordinating office for multi-agency cases, developing a risk-flagging digital system for holistic child views, and reviewing criteria for involuntary child removal to balance protection with parental rights. The Ministry will also publish clearer guidelines on intervention thresholds, introduce protective reporting mechanisms, and track improvements via metrics such as reduced caseloads and faster response times.

results.title: Low Ridership of City Direct Services 675, 676 and 677 and Strategies for Traffic Optimisation
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that evening peak ridership for City Direct Services 675, 676, and 677 is generally lower than morning peak levels. While the Ministry is currently monitoring these services, there are no immediate plans to adjust routes, frequencies, or operational timings. Consequently, the Ministry will not be implementing traffic measures or reducing bus stops to improve travel speeds or journey times at this time.

results.title: Making Criteria of Silver Support Scheme More Flexible for Initially Ineligible Seniors
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Silver Support Scheme targets seniors with lower historical incomes and limited family support, noting that Total CPF Contributions at age 55 reflect past earnings regardless of subsequent withdrawals for housing or medical needs. While the TCC threshold doubled to $140,000 in 2021 and the household income limit rose to $2,300 in 2025, the scheme does not automatically adjust for seniors who depleted savings after age 55. However, seniors facing unique circumstances may write to the CPF Board for a holistic, case-by-case eligibility review. Those ineligible may also seek assistance from their nearest Social Service Office.

results.title: Mandatory First Aid and AED Training and Certification in Secondary Four School Syllabus
results.summary: Basic first aid, cardiopulmonary resuscitation, and automated external defibrillator (AED) training are already integrated into Physical Education lessons for upper primary and lower secondary students in Ministry of Education schools. Uniformed group Co-Curricular Activities also include this training in their programmes. The Ministry of Education and the Singapore Civil Defence Force will regularly review the adequacy of these existing training provisions. The Minister did not confirm the incorporation of certification-focused training into the Secondary Four syllabus as requested.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Equivalent Corporate Governance Standards Abroad for SGX-listed Entities
results.summary: SGX-listed entities must maintain equivalent governance standards overseas by implementing effective internal controls, risk management systems, and codes of conduct for all operations. Boards are required to assess and remediate significant weaknesses in foreign operations and ensure timely disclosure of investigations under the Securities and Futures Act (SFA) and SGX Listing Rules. Intentional or reckless non-compliance with disclosure obligations attracts fines up to $250,000 and/or imprisonment of up to seven years. The Monetary Authority of Singapore and SGX RegCo collaborate to enforce these obligations, issuing public queries or taking firm action against infringements to preserve capital market transparency. Entities must also comply with applicable laws in their operating jurisdictions and cooperate with relevant authorities.

results.title: Measures to Investigate and Prevent Circulation of Illegal Obscene Materials
results.summary: The Online Criminal Harms Act (OCHA), introduced in 2023, empowers the Government to direct online service providers to block access to illegal obscene materials involving minors and to supply information for investigations. The Ministry of Digital Development and Information and the Ministry of Law have tabled the Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill to establish a new Online Safety Commission (OSC). This Commission will support victims, including minors, facing harms like intimate image abuse by issuing directions to stop the circulation of such materials upon receiving a report.

results.title: Mechanisms in FIT-P to Address Protectionism from Major Economies
results.summary: The Future of Investment and Trade (FIT) Partnership, launched on 16 September 2025, addresses rising protectionism by uniting trade-dependent nations to uphold a rules-based multilateral system. The initiative focuses on three pillars: strengthening supply chain resilience, facilitating trade and investment, and advancing a positive trade agenda through outcome-oriented governance. A key operational feature allows subsets of members to advance shared interests without requiring full consensus, enabling faster progress on specific initiatives. Singapore will host the first Ministerial meeting in November 2025 to adopt ready deliverables and a detailed work programme. Over time, successful initiatives will be scaled to wider multilateral platforms like the World Trade Organization or adopted by broader partners, with private sector collaboration ensuring practical impact on real business challenges.

results.title: Median PSLE Achievement Level Scores by Housing Type
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that the correlation between higher socio-economic status and better PSLE performance has remained stable over the past five years. However, Singapore students from lower socio-economic backgrounds outperform the average OECD student across all domains per OECD PISA 2022 data. The Ministry plans to address equity through enhanced preschool accessibility, targeted support for high-need students, and community wraparound services. No specific numerical median scores by housing type were disclosed, nor were detailed timelines for interventions provided.

results.title: Mitigation Actions Taken to Protect At-risk Children from Abusive Situations against Potential System Lapses
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development addressed queries regarding child protection measures, noting that the Domestic Violence Awareness Training review and sector expansion plans are under consideration. The Ministry is evaluating guidelines on social worker caseloads to prevent burnout and is exploring data integration with multi-agency records, including school absenteeism and police reports, to identify at-risk children. Legislative proposals to impose a duty on parents and teachers to report breaches of care orders, alongside empowering officers to issue immediate protective directions, are being considered. Additionally, steps to educate agencies on making police reports for abused children and expected police actions upon such reports are being outlined following the Review Panel's findings published on 23 October 2025.

results.title: Monitoring Compliance to Workplace Fairness Act Requirements and Educating Companies on Statutory Tort of Discrimination
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower monitors workplace discrimination effectiveness through regular surveys and its annual Employment Standards Report, which will soon track pre- and in-employment claims under the Workplace Fairness Act. To ensure employer familiarity with the statutory tort of discrimination, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices launched monthly briefings in September 2025. TAFEP partners with industry bodies to conduct outreach and is developing comprehensive resources, including step-by-step guides, templates, and HR e-learning modules. These materials are designed to support small- and medium-sized enterprises and companies lacking dedicated HR personnel in integrating compliance into their corporate training programmes.

results.title: Monitoring Foreign-linked Fundraising Events and Activities to Align with Singapore's Principles of Neutrality
results.summary: The Commissioner of Charities reviews permit applications for Fund-raising for Foreign Charitable Purposes with input from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Key considerations include ensuring activities abide by Singapore and international law to align with national principles of neutrality and a rules-based international order. The Ministry monitors foreign-linked fundraising events to prevent political or humanitarian implications abroad that could conflict with these commitments. No specific timeline or new operational constraints were stated in the response.

results.title: NRF to Conduct National Research Challenge towards Cost-Effective Elderly Access Solutions for Older HDB Estates with Shortage of Direct Lift Access
results.summary: The Minister stated that since the 2001 Lift Upgrading Programme, HDB has upgraded over 5,000 blocks, achieving direct lift access on every floor in approximately 99% of estates through innovations like machine room-less and bubble lifts. To address remaining infrastructure gaps, HDB will pilot new lift technologies with research institutes and launch the HDB Cool Ideas Enterprise in the first half of 2026. This upcoming platform will provide enterprises with funding, mentorship, and testbed facilities to co-develop cost-effective solutions for older HDB estates lacking direct lift access, continuing the board's exploration of innovative approaches to improve residents' quality of life.

results.title: Number of Carbon Offset Credits Purchased by Singapore Annually
results.summary: As of the current date, the Government and carbon tax-liable companies have not purchased any carbon credits under the International Carbon Credits Framework. In September 2025, the Government announced plans to contract high-quality nature-based carbon credits from four projects located in Ghana, Paraguay, and Peru. These initiatives are expected to deliver 2.175 million tonnes of carbon credits to support Singapore's 2030 Nationally Determined Contribution. No credits have been purchased from the region specified in the question to date.

results.title: Measures for Prompt Police Case Resolution and Mandating Assignment of Police Officers with Child Protection Training in Child-related Cases
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development accepts all Review Panel recommendations and will implement them by the end of 2026, while the Singapore Police Force (SPF) does not track average case closure durations due to varying offence complexities. Regarding the February 2020 fatal child abuse case, the SPF confirmed that the Investigation Officer and supervisor failed to escalate the missing vulnerable person case to regular review sessions, resulting in the officer's resignation. The SPF maintains that missing vulnerable persons are accorded priority and escalated to regular case review sessions regardless of missing duration, supported by internal controls including daily case reviews and multiple supervision levels. The Ministry will work with the sector to address reporting protocols, risk assessment, and practitioner support, with no intention to point fingers but issuing apologies to affected agencies for any perceived shortcomings.

results.title: Outcomes of EatWise SG Programme and Nutrition Support Access for Vulnerable Seniors
results.summary: The Minister for Health directed that the question regarding EatWise SG programme outcomes and measures for vulnerable seniors be addressed via a written answer. This response was provided in the oral reply to Parliamentary Question No 43 during the Parliament Sitting on 4 November 2025. No additional details on specific outcomes, metrics, or support mechanisms were stated in this transcript.

results.title: Plans for BlueSG's EV Charging Points in Light of Winding Down
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority is discussing with current and alternative operators to ensure EV charging points in HDB estates remain available for public use following BlueSG's winding down. This arrangement confirms the Government remains on track to deploy 60,000 EV charging points across Singapore by 2030. No specific mitigation steps beyond these ongoing discussions were detailed in the response.

results.title: Potential Health Risks of Singaporeans Seeking Overseas Postnatal Confinement Care
results.summary: The Ministry of Health clarified that postnatal confinement services, such as nourishment and massages, are not classified as healthcare services and therefore fall outside the scope of the Healthcare Services Act. Consequently, the Ministry does not monitor these services, track their usage trends, or have plans to institute specific standards or accreditation for them. Hospitals retain responsibility for ensuring maternal and newborn discharge only when medically stable, with follow-up care managed through standard medical reviews and vaccinations rather than regulating private confinement arrangements.

results.title: Potential Mental Health Impact Associated with Second-hand Smoke Exposure
results.summary: The Minister for Health stated that while some studies suggest an association between second-hand smoke exposure and poor mental health, there is currently insufficient evidence to confirm that such exposure causes adverse mental health outcomes. Consequently, the Government does not have specific studies under review to assess this potential impact. The Minister noted that commonly recognised risk factors for mental health issues remain lifestyle aspects, including stress, sleep problems, chronic diseases, and excessive screen time.

results.title: Profile of Singaporeans in Bankruptcy
results.summary: The Minister for Law reported on Singaporeans made bankrupt between 2021 and August 2025, noting that nearly half were aged 40 to 54. Data from Statements of Affairs indicate that over two-thirds were employed and almost two-thirds were married with immediate family dependents at the time of bankruptcy. Furthermore, more than 80% of these bankrupts have been paying their monthly contributions. The Minister did not provide specific percentages for the average profile of bankrupts regarding employment or family status beyond these aggregate figures.

results.title: Progress in Developing Diploma Courses and Internships to Prepare for Meaningful Local Agri-food Sector Careers
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency partners with Institutes of Higher Learning and local farms to deliver Pre-employment Training (PET) and Continuing Education and Training (CET) programmes. As of October 2025, eight PET and CET programmes alongside SkillsFuture short courses are available to equip students and adult job seekers with relevant agri-food sector skills. These initiatives provide structured internship opportunities through polytechnic-farm matching, offering first-hand industry experience. Additionally, the programmes incorporate cross-disciplinary skills to enhance graduate adaptability and open employment opportunities across multiple sectors, addressing sector volatility by fostering versatility.

results.title: Prohibiting Employer Requests for Last-drawn Salary Information during Recruitment to Promote Fair Wage Practices
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will not prohibit employers from requesting last-drawn salary information during recruitment, as such data may help gauge seniority and inform appropriate offers. While the Ministry acknowledges that heavy reliance on prior pay risks anchoring offers to levels that do not reflect current skills, jobseekers remain unobliged to disclose past salaries. Consequently, candidates may negotiate compensation based on their skills, experience, and the specific demands of the role rather than historical pay data.

results.title: Projected Population Density Targets Incorporating Spatial Comfort Factors
results.summary: The Government does not plan around a single optimum population density for Singapore. Instead, it adopts a long-term approach to land use and infrastructure planning that considers changing demographic trends and Singaporeans' evolving needs. To ensure a liveable environment, urban design strategies are incorporated islandwide, including varying building heights and creating well-connected, pedestrian-friendly public spaces. These measures aim to provide sufficient land for current and future needs while maintaining a high-quality living environment for all residents.

results.title: Property and Train Damage Caused by Use of Personal Mobility Aids on Public Transport
results.summary: No cases of deliberate property damage by Personal Mobility Aid (PMA) users have been reported, though accidental incidents occur. Transport workers are empowered to turn away errant commuters, such as those with oversized PMAs, and may request Police assistance for uncooperative passengers. The Land Transport Authority will align PMA dimension limits on public paths with those on public transport to reduce oversized aid usage. Public education on these amended rules will be implemented in 2026 to support enforcement against errant users.

results.title: Proportion of Seniors Screened or Flagged for Malnutrition
results.summary: Questions regarding the proportion of seniors screened for malnutrition, nutritional indicators at hawker centres, and personalised advice under Healthier SG were raised. The Minister for Health noted that these queries were addressed in the answer to Question No 43 during the Parliament Sitting on 4 November 2025. No new data, timelines, or specific policy measures were introduced in this session. Readers seeking the detailed responses should refer to the record of the 4 November 2025 sitting.

results.title: Proposal for Dog Run Area on State Land near Serangoon North View
results.summary: The Minister for Law stated that the Government has no current plans to designate a dog run park on the state land parcel adjacent to Serangoon North View. This response directly addresses the proposal for a dog run area on state land near Serangoon North View. No alternative timeline or future review mechanism was mentioned in the parliamentary exchange.

results.title: Providing Requested Hardcopy Utility Bills Free for Next Three Years and Late-payment Fee Waivers for Affected Customers
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority (EMA) requires SP Services to provide free hardcopy utility bills upon request and will waive late-payment fees for customers affected by the recent transition to electronic billing. EMA does not mandate all electricity retailers to offer hardcopy bills, as the Open Electricity Market allows retailers to set their own billing options, including electronic-only services or charging fees for paper copies. Consumers retain the choice of retailers and must review terms and conditions regarding billing arrangements before signing up. EMA requires all retailers to provide a standardised factsheet detailing their specific billing arrangements and key terms to consumers.

results.title: Rationale for Business Adaptation Grant Cap and Duration and Assessment of Scheme's Impact and Success
results.summary: The Business Adaptation Grant (BizAdapt) launched on 7 October 2025 provides a capped $100,000 per company for two years, ending 6 October 2027, to assist firms adapting to new tariff environments. The cap reflects internal assessments of necessary third-party advisory services for supply chain reconfiguration. The Ministry plans to assess the scheme's impact by surveying businesses on their ability to evaluate tariff effects and review supply chains. Results will determine if further support is required.

results.title: Rationale for Ending Fostering Arrangements at Age 16
results.summary: The Minister clarified that fostering arrangements do not automatically end at age 16 under the Children and Young Persons Act (CYPA) 1993. Eligibility extends to all children and young persons below 18 requiring protection or alternative care. Furthermore, if a foster youth remains in school or is not yet ready for independent living, the arrangement may continue until age 21 to ensure stability during the transition to adulthood. This policy allows for extended support beyond the standard age limit based on individual readiness.

results.title: Reasons for Low Take-up Rate of $500 SkillsFuture Credit Top-up
results.summary: The Government provided a one-off $500 SkillsFuture Credit top-up to eligible Singaporeans aged 25 and above in 2020, which expires by the end of 2025 to encourage timely upskilling. As of end-September 2025, approximately 800,000 individuals, or three in ten eligible Singaporeans, have utilized the credit, with mid-career workers aged 40 to 60 comprising nearly half of this group. Low take-up is attributed to employer-sponsored training covering about 241,000 individuals in 2024 and the opportunity cost for working adults. To address this, SkillsFuture Singapore has enhanced the MySkillsFuture portal's user interface, introduced flexible e-learning modalities like bite-sized modules, and plans further integration of course search and sign-up. The Government will not extend the credit's expiry but will continue guiding learners toward in-demand skills such as artificial intelligence.

results.title: Reasons for Non-disclosure of Identities of Five Content Creators Issued Advisory Letters for Providing Non-licensed Financial Advice
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance stated that the question regarding five content creators issued advisory letters by MAS for unlicensed financial advice was previously addressed in a parliamentary reply on 4 November 2025. The response did not disclose the identities or specific content of the advice, nor did it provide clear examples of non-compliant content for public reference. No new considerations or examples were presented during this session.

results.title: Reducing National Water Consumption Rate in Non-domestic Sector
results.summary: PUB manages national water consumption via right-pricing, mandatory efficiency plans for large users, and specific requirements for new projects exceeding 60,000 m³ in wafer fabrication, electronics, biomedical, and pharmaceutical sectors effective 1 January 2024. New plants in front-end semiconductor manufacturing must achieve a minimum 50% recycling rate, with non-compliance attracting a maximum penalty of $40,000. PUB reviews sectoral benchmarks annually to align with industry trends and technology, while supporting efficiency projects through the Water Efficiency Fund and grants. Exceptional performance is recognized via the Singapore Watermark Awards, and companies are guided by best practice guides to continually enhance water efficiency.

results.title: Rental Structure Analysis for Socially-Conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres
results.summary: Since 2019, the National Environment Agency (NEA) has capped stall rentals at Socially-Conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres (SEHCs) to the average of independently valued rents. Currently, only one operator applies a formula tying rent to monthly gross turnover, subject to a strict cap of $2,550. The Minister did not disclose the specific percentage range of gross turnover charged or the overall share of turnover-based rents relative to total rents. Consequently, the precise range and proportion of turnover rents remain untracked in the provided response.

results.title: Reported Statistics of Victims of Human Trafficking
results.summary: In 2024, the Singapore Police Force and Ministry of Manpower investigated 45 alleged trafficking cases involving 53 victims, comprising 40 sex trafficking and five labour trafficking offences. None of these victims were linked to overseas scam-related recruitment activities. Authorities are currently investigating one Singapore-registered company regarding illicit overseas recruitment for scams, though further details remain undisclosed as the investigation is ongoing. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided these statistics during the parliamentary session.

results.title: Reports of Ransomware Incidents by Singapore-based Companies and Legislation on Ban of Ransom Payments
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, an average of 141 ransomware incidents were reported annually to Singapore government agencies, with approximately 60% involving small and medium enterprises. The Ministry for Digital Development and Information strongly discourages ransom payments, noting they do not guarantee system restoration and may encourage repeat attacks. While acknowledging that jurisdictions like the United Kingdom are considering legislative bans to cut criminal funding, the Ministry is concerned payments could shift underground. Consequently, Singapore is continuing engagements with counterparts to assess the effectiveness of such bans before deciding on legislation, while encouraging adoption of good cyber hygiene practices via the Ransomware Portal.

results.title: Retail Food Establishments Which Ceased Operations After Five Years or Less That Have Recorded Profit
results.summary: As of 23 October 2025, 63% of retail food establishments closed within five years or less were registered under this category. Among these short-lived businesses, 82% had never recorded a profit in their annual tax declarations. The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry provided these statistics regarding establishments closed between 1 January and 23 October 2025. No further details on the proportion of profitable closures were stated in the response.

results.title: Review Limit of Non-training Days for Trainee Lawyers and Enforcement Measures against Non-compliant Law Firms
results.summary: The Practice Training Framework, administered by the Singapore Institute of Legal Education (SILE) since 2024, permits a maximum of 18 non-training days during the 12-month Practice Training Period (PTP). Trainees exceeding this limit may take additional leave provided they make up the shortfall within 16 months from the PTP start date, a departure from pre-2024 rules requiring make-up days within the PTP itself. While the 18-day benchmark aligns with industry norms and other Commonwealth jurisdictions, the specific number of leave days remains negotiable between the trainee and supervising law practice. The Ministry of Law will monitor the framework's efficacy starting with the first batch admitted in April 2025, with potential reviews contingent on industry feedback regarding the appropriateness of the current limit.

results.title: Reviewing Conduct of Insurers with Unreasonable Behaviour when Assessing Claims by Accident Victims
results.summary: The Minister for Finance directed Members to refer to the Monetary Authority of Singapore's response to similar questions filed on 4 November 2025 regarding insurer conduct and regulatory frameworks. This response addresses concerns about unreasonable behaviour in assessing accident victim claims and overly litigious actions against vulnerable third-party claimants. No new measures, timelines, or specific regulatory changes were announced in this session. The detailed policy signals and operational constraints remain contained within the referenced prior response.

results.title: Reviewing Criteria for Government Scholarships
results.summary: The Public Service selects scholarship candidates based on academic results alongside community service, co-curricular achievements, psychometric tests, and assessments of values, perseverance, curiosity, and adaptability. Awards are actively extended to students from polytechnics, Integrated Programme schools, and junior colleges to foster a diverse talent pool. The Government will continue reviewing selection criteria to ensure they effectively identify outstanding Singaporeans from varied backgrounds for public service roles.

results.title: Reviewing Manpower Policies to Better Protect Full-time Workers
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that employees on fixed-term contracts are protected under existing legislation, including the Employment Act, Central Provident Fund Act, and Work Injury Compensation Act. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) is conducting an ongoing tripartite review of the Employment Act to assess protections for various worker groups, including those on fixed-term contracts. MOM will continue monitoring workforce needs to ensure appropriate support is provided. The response did not specify a timeline for the review or new policy measures beyond the ongoing study.

results.title: Reviewing Multi-storey Car Parks' Rooftop Urban Farms' Contribution to Local Food Production
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency and Housing Development Board tendered 16 Multi-Storey Car Park (MSCP) sites for pilot commercial urban farming in 2020 and 2021, with only seven remaining today. These farms contribute less than 1% of local food production in 2024, reflecting limited employment outcomes and profitability. While farms facilitate community engagement, residents report disamenities like noise and odour, prompting SFA investigations and mitigation measures. No new tenders for MSCP commercial farming are planned; interested entities should instead participate in SFA's land and sea space tenders, which provide greater space and longer leases to support local production and food security.

results.title: Reviewing Plans and Delivery Timelines for Support Infrastructure in Light of Accelerated BTO Launches
results.summary: In response to accelerated Build-to-Order (BTO) launches, the Government is reviewing planning and delivery timelines for supporting infrastructure, including childcare centres, schools, transport links, and community amenities. The Ministry of National Development established an inter-agency BTO Coordination Committee to examine detailed planning parameters and delivery schedules, specifically targeting improvements for future large-scale estates and early-phase residents. Agencies such as the Land Transport Authority, Early Childhood Development Agency, and Ministry of Education continue to coordinate closely with the Housing and Development Board to ensure timely delivery of required services and amenities for new residents.

results.title: Reviewing Public Transport Fare Adjustment Formula
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport clarified that the Public Transport Council (PTC) sets fares based on a formula reflecting service costs, national wage growth, core inflation, and energy prices. For 2025, the PTC determined a maximum allowable fare adjustment of 14.4%, comprising a 1.5% formula output and a 12.9% deferred quantum from previous years, resulting in a final 5% increase funded partly by government subsidy. There is no limit to carrying forward deferred fare quantum, as evidenced by the reduction from 15.6% in 2023 to 9.4% today. The Minister noted the formula aims to balance commuter affordability with system financial sustainability.
