results.title: Security Risks of Using China-made Drones and Procurement Safeguards for National Safety and Cybersecurity
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence procures drones based on SAF operational needs, with all equipment assessed for suitability before deployment. Security and safety requirements are integral to these assessments. For equipment connected to military networks, rigorous cybersecurity evaluations are conducted to safeguard national safety. The Minister did not specify if any China-linked drones are currently used in military infrastructure or detail specific procurement measures beyond these general assessment criteria.

results.title: Sentencing Consistency following Issuance of Singapore Medical Council Sentencing Guidelines
results.summary: Post-2019 reforms to the Singapore Medical Council (SMC) disciplinary process, enacted via the Medical Registration Act 1997 amendments on 1 July 2022, established an independent Disciplinary Commission (DC) to convene and oversee Disciplinary Tribunals (DTs). The DC ensures consistency and proportionality by training DT members on natural justice, due process, and sentencing principles within the SMC Sentencing Guidelines, case precedents, and Court of Three Judges (C3J) appeal judgements. Operational enhancements include an Inquiry Committee to assess complaint merit, a six-year time-bar for incidents known to the complainant, and legislated timelines for decisions to expedite case closure. These measures collectively enhance transparency and predictability in SMC disciplinary outcomes while allowing DT members to exercise autonomous judgment based on professional expertise and relevant mitigating circumstances.

results.title: Stakeholder Engagement Plan for VERS and Measures to Prevent Speculative Flat Purchases
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development and the Housing and Development Board will engage Singaporeans to gather views on the Voluntary Early Redevelopment Scheme (VERS) framework and policy parameters. Specific details regarding the engaged groups, organisations, and the communication strategy to discourage speculative flat purchases for VERS profiteering will be shared when ready. No specific timelines, eligible groups, or operational constraints were provided in the response.

results.title: Statistics for MICE Sector in Past Three Years Compared to Pre-COVID-19 Numbers
results.summary: MICE visitor arrivals reached 830,000 in 2023 and 1.1 million in 2024, surpassing the 2019 pre-pandemic figure of 730,000, with 560,000 arrivals recorded in the first half of 2025 indicating sustained growth. To address rising costs and enhance competitiveness, the Government is studying a new downtown MICE hub to expand capacity and leisure offerings. The Singapore Tourism Board (STB) supports the sector through the Business Events in Singapore (BEiS) scheme, which provides facilitation and funding for quality events, alongside programmes designed to help businesses adopt efficient technologies, streamline operations, and upskill workers to reduce long-term operating costs. The Government continually reviews these schemes to ensure Singapore remains an attractive global MICE destination.

results.title: Studies on Effect of Forested Areas towards Water Flows and Rising Water Levels in Areas between Tampines Road and Serangoon East Dam
results.summary: The Public Utilities Board assessed the Sungei Serangoon drainage system, including the area between Tampines Road and Serangoon East Dam, as adequate based on the Urban Redevelopment Authority's Master Plan. This assessment confirms the system can withstand water level rises from storm water runoff associated with current or future developments in the forested areas. Consequently, the need for specific studies on how forested areas stabilize water flow into the Serangoon Reservoir was not addressed in the response.

results.title: Support Measures for Families with Young Children Spending Extended Hours in Childcare
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development highlighted that parents serve as children's primary teachers and encouraged daily bonding routines to support development. Employers are urged to provide family-friendly environments, supported by the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests effective 1 December 2024, which formalize processes for one-off or sustained caregiving requests. The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) prioritizes preschool quality and holistic child development, having updated national learning frameworks to emphasize social and emotional competencies. Additionally, ECDA provides educators with resources and training on social-emotional development to support children in full-day childcare settings. The response notes a trend toward full-day enrolment to balance work and family commitments but does not specify new financial subsidies or detailed mitigation steps beyond these structural and educational measures.

results.title: Supporting Teachers with Limited Public Transport Options to Reach School on Time
results.summary: The Minister confirmed all schools start no earlier than 7.30 am, with MOE generally deploying teachers within one hour of travel by public transport. Teachers may request posting changes to nearer schools, which MOE will support where possible. Schools have autonomy to implement flexible work arrangements, such as later reporting times, if operationally feasible and no morning lessons or duties exist. Persistent delays due to transport issues will trigger a further review to determine best support measures.

results.title: Suspension of Standing Orders to Allow Minister of State and Minister for Law to Speak More Than Once and To Remove Time Limit
results.summary: The Minister of State for Digital Development and Information and the Minister for Law seek permission to speak twice and exceed standard time limits during the Second Reading of the Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill. This request amends Standing Order No 48(3) to allow multiple speeches and Standing Order 48(8) to remove time limits for their respective contributions on harms, statutory torts, and end-user identification. The Minister of State for Digital Development and Information will cover the scope of harms, while the Minister for Law addresses statutory torts and anonymity issues. The mover, Minister Josephine Teo, retains the right to open and close the debate as per standard procedure.

results.title: Takedown of Self-harm and Suicidal Content on Social Media Channels
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that under the Broadcasting Act, the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) can direct social media services and app stores to disable access for Singapore users to content advocating or instructing on self-harm or suicide, including on gaming apps. This authority extends to large groups enabling online interactions, treating them similarly to non-private communications. For private messages containing such content, the IMDA's Code of Practice mandates easily accessible user reporting mechanisms, allowing individuals to block or report senders. The Government will continue updating regulatory efforts to promote safe online spaces as the digital landscape evolves.

results.title: Tangible Creative Breakthroughs Resulting from Students Topping PISA Assessments in Creative Thinking
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that while Singaporean students consistently perform well in PISA 21st Century Competencies like creative thinking, the Government has no plans to conduct longitudinal studies tracking these students. This decision stems from the view that future innovations depend on multiple factors beyond performance at age 15. Consequently, the Government will not monitor whether strong PISA creative thinking scores specifically lead to tangible future breakthroughs.

results.title: Towards a Safer, Plastics-lite Singapore
results.summary: MP Poh Li San proposed two policy measures to reduce single-use plastics: government grants for refill stations and a Green Mark rating for F&B operators offering Bring Your Own (BYO) discounts. She highlighted that Singapore's plastic recycling rate is only 5% due to high costs, urging a shift toward reuse and prevention. The Minister acknowledged these suggestions, noting existing initiatives like the Disposable Carrier Bag Charge, Packaging Partnership Programme, and SG Eco Fund already support reuse and recycling. He confirmed the Beverage Containers Return Scheme will launch on 1 April 2026 to incentivise returning plastic and metal containers. While the Government encourages private sector participation in BYO campaigns, it noted that specific provisions for responding to the MP's proposals were not available during the session.

results.title: Tracking of Degree and Diploma Courses with Consistent Poor Wage Outcomes
results.summary: The Ministry of Education monitors graduate employment outcomes via the annual Graduate Employment Survey conducted six months post-graduation. Fluctuations in rates are attributed to labour market conditions, economic cycles, and sector-specific trends. The Minister stated there is no degree or diploma course that consistently ranks among the lowest for full-time permanent employment or median salaries. Consequently, no specific list of courses with consistently poor wage outcomes or employment rates has been identified or published.

results.title: Train Loading Operating Performance Standards and Maximum Load during Peak Hours
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that current Operating Performance Standards (OPS) mandate MRT operators to maintain average peak-hour train loading below five persons per square metre. Data from September 2025 showed no line exceeded this threshold. Furthermore, based on planned capacity upgrades and network expansions, the government does not anticipate any MRT line exceeding the OPS for train loading by 2030.

results.title: Transparency Requirements and Compliance Reviews for Foreign-Linked Charitable Organisations Operating in Singapore
results.summary: Entities raising funds in Singapore for foreign charitable causes must obtain a Fund-raising for Foreign Charitable Purposes permit from the Commissioner of Charities (COC) before starting appeals. The COC reviews applications with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Ministry of Home Affairs to ensure legitimacy and alignment with foreign policy. Permit details are published on the Charity Portal, and holders must adhere to submitted conditions, provide accurate donor information, and submit a Statement of Accounts post-appeal. This framework ensures compliance with Singapore laws while maintaining transparency for cross-border fundraising activities.

results.title: Unequal Enforcement against Nuisance-markers in Neighbour Disputes due to Restricting CRU Pilot to Tampines
results.summary: Restricting the Community Relations Unit (CRU) pilot to Tampines does not result in unequal enforcement, as grassroots leaders and frontline agencies continue managing neighbour disputes across all towns. Residents outside Tampines will still benefit from enhancements to the Community Disputes Management Framework, including improved processes and powers for Community Disputes Resolution Tribunals. The Ministry of Law is operationalising these tribunal enhancements as soon as practicable.

results.title: Unfair Clauses in Government Tender Specifications
results.summary: The Ministry of Finance (MOF) clarified that autonomous universities like SUSS operate independently but must adhere to fundamental procurement principles. To address SME concerns, MOF made unlimited liability clauses optional in the Standard Consultancy Agreement (SCA) in November 2024, with mandatory implementation starting 1 December 2025. Government agencies are expected to pay for additional services not covered by contracts and typically make monthly progress payments to construction contractors. MOF has also introduced Tender Lite for procurements up to $1 million to simplify access for SMEs. The Government encourages collaborative, win-win contracting and will review standard clauses if feedback indicates they are unfair or too onerous.

results.title: Update on Inter-agency Efforts to Detect, Track and Neutralise Unauthorised Unmanned Aircraft System Activities Near Aiports
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs coordinates inter-agency efforts to detect, track, and neutralise unauthorised unmanned aircraft systems (UAS) near Changi and Seletar Airports, including threats from offshore waters. The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore and Changi Airport Group enforce a 5-kilometre No-Fly Zone and deploy in-situ counter-UAS systems for direct threat management. Established protocols involving the Singapore Police Force, Singapore Armed Forces, and Maritime & Port Authority govern incident response, with frontline officers equipped with handheld jammers. The Strategic Location Response deployment, announced in June, enhances swift responses to public security incidents. While these multi-layered measures are regularly exercised, the Ministry notes that security remains a constant effort due to evolving technology and human factors.

results.title: Updated Data on Yearly Amount of Plastic Waste Produced by Singapore since 2019
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2024, plastic waste generation fluctuated, with 2020–2021 figures potentially skewed by the COVID-19 pandemic. Following pandemic easing, waste levels rose briefly before trending downwards. Under the Resource Sustainability Act and Zero Waste Masterplan, new schemes including the Disposable Carrier Bag Charge and Beverage Container Return Scheme will be implemented next year to further reduce plastic waste disposal.

results.title: Updates on Hawkers Development Programme and Exploration of Potential Partnerships with Food-related Courses at IHLs
results.summary: The National Environment Agency is reviewing the Hawkers' Development Programme to ensure its continued relevance for aspiring hawkers. An update regarding the review's completion and the timing of the next intake will be provided once the assessment is finalized. The Minister did not confirm whether partnerships with polytechnics and ITEs offering food-related courses will be explored as part of the programme. Details on the review's specific outcomes or any new partnership initiatives remain pending.

results.title: Utilisation Rates of SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit (SFEC) and Measures to Encourage SFEC Use before Expiry in 2026
results.summary: Approximately 40,000 enterprises, or half of eligible firms, have utilised the SkillsFuture Enterprise Credit (SFEC) since its 2020 introduction. The credit, originally expiring in June 2025, has been extended until the second half of 2026 to allow more time for usage. Under the redesigned scheme launching in the second half of 2026, employers will receive a fresh $10,000 in credits via an online wallet to immediately offset out-of-pocket costs for eligible workforce transformation initiatives. This shift from a reimbursement basis to an upfront credit aims to ease cash flow concerns for employers investing in employee capabilities.

results.title: Vacancy Rate for Nursing Positions in Restructured Hospitals
results.summary: As of end-September 2025, public hospitals reported over 900 nursing vacancies in the most recent three months, though the Minister noted the vacancy rate is not a meaningful metric due to ongoing system expansion and proactive hiring for new facilities like Eastern General Hospital. Approximately 1,000 local nursing graduates were hired by clusters this year, matching previous years, yet recruitment continues alongside foreign hires to meet a target of growing national healthcare manpower by 20% by 2030. The Ministry attributes current gaps to applicant preferences for specific locations or shifts that do not align with institutional needs, encouraging graduates to consider varied work conditions. Consequently, foreign nurses will continue to supplement local supply as local graduates alone cannot fulfil future capacity requirements.

results.title: Weekly Average Number of Classroom Hours Spent by Teachers from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding the average weekly classroom instructional hours for teachers from 2021 to 2025 was previously addressed via a written answer to Parliamentary Question No 40 on 14 October 2025. No new data, trends, or policy changes were provided during this session. The specific figures and any evolution over the past five years remain available only in the referenced written response.

results.title: Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill
results.summary: The Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Bill establishes the Office of the Commissioner of Online Safety (OSC) to address online harms, with the agency set to commence operations in the first half of 2026. The legislation introduces a Statutory Torts Framework enabling victims to seek civil remedies against perpetrators while maintaining platforms as the primary reporting channel, except for egregious cases like intimate image abuse which allow direct OSC reporting. The OSC will issue directions to remove harmful content based on a "reasonable grounds to believe" threshold rather than mere suspicion, balancing speedy intervention with due process. While the Bill defines 13 categories of harm and empowers the Commissioner to issue orders, debates highlighted concerns regarding the evidentiary thresholds, potential for excessive litigation, and the need for robust safeguards against misuse of disclosed identity information.
